name: Horn Gjallarhorn
description: The Gjallarhorn is a mythical horn in Norse mythology, most famously associated with the god Heimdallr. It is described as a resounding horn whose blast can be heard throughout all the Nine Realms, signaling the arrival of Ragnarök, the end of the world. The origins of the Gjallarhorn are somewhat obscure, but it is generally understood to be a significant instrument of warning and defense. Heimdallr, as the watchman of Asgard, guards the Bifrost bridge and uses the Gjallarhorn to alert the gods and heroes of impending danger. The horn's sound is said to be so powerful that it can awaken all the warriors and deities, preparing them for the final battle against the giants and monsters. The Gjallarhorn is more than just a horn; it represents vigilance, preparedness, and the inevitability of fate. Its possession signifies a responsibility to protect the cosmos from destruction. The echoes of the Gjallarhorn serve as a reminder of the cyclical nature of existence in Norse cosmology – destruction leading to renewal. Some interpretations suggest it also signifies the spread of knowledge and awareness.
subtitle: The Resounding Horn of Ragnarök
origin: Origin unknown, possibly crafted by dwarves or bestowed upon Heimdallr.

keyMyths.title: Heimdallr's Warning
keyMyths.description: Heimdallr will blow the Gjallarhorn to signal the start of Ragnarök, alerting the gods and heroes to prepare for the final battle.

keyMyths.title: Sound Across Nine Realms
keyMyths.description: The Gjallarhorn's sound is so powerful that it can be heard throughout all the Nine Realms, reaching gods, humans, and monsters alike.

sources.author: Snorri Sturluson
sources.description: A key source for Norse mythology, including descriptions of Heimdallr and the Gjallarhorn.
sources.text: A key source for Norse mythology, including descriptions of Heimdallr and the Gjallarhorn.

sources.author: Various Authors
sources.description: A collection of Old Norse poems, some of which reference the events of Ragnarök and Heimdallr's role.
